verificationNote: Indicative requirements for SOC 2 Security (Common Criteria). Not verbatim AICPA text.

requirements.requirement: Demonstrate commitment to integrity and ethical values through a code of conduct, ethics training, and enforcement mechanisms.
Attract, develop, and retain competent individuals with security-relevant skills through hiring, training, and performance management.
Conduct periodic risk assessments to identify threats and vulnerabilities to the system, analysing likelihood and impact.
Implement ongoing monitoring and periodic evaluations to assess the design and operating effectiveness of internal controls.
Deploy IT general controls over technology infrastructure including access management, change management, and operations.
Implement logical access security controls including authentication, authorization, and access monitoring.
Authorize, modify, and remove access to systems and data based on defined roles and responsibilities, with periodic access reviews.
Implement boundary protection controls to prevent, detect, and respond to threats from external sources.
Protect data in transit through encryption and restrict unauthorized data movement.
Monitor systems for anomalous behaviour indicative of security incidents using SIEM and alerting mechanisms.
Maintain and execute an incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents.
Implement a formal change management process with authorization, testing, approval, and documentation for all system changes.
Identify, assess, and mitigate risks from potential business disruptions including disaster recovery and business continuity.
Assess and manage risks associated with vendors and business partners through due diligence and ongoing monitoring.
